```bash
cat <<'EOF' > ~/.jupyter/jupyter_notebook_config.py
c = get_config()

# Server configuration
c.NotebookApp.ip = '*'
c.NotebookApp.port = 8888
c.NotebookApp.open_browser = False
c.NotebookApp.allow_root = True

# Password authentication
c.NotebookApp.password = 'sha1:1c1f699b19ed30e9fe8105cdc51cfc302580b0d8'

# SSL configuration
c.NotebookApp.certfile = '/root/.jupyter/cert.pem'
c.NotebookApp.keyfile = '/root/.jupyter/cert.key'

# Disable token authentication since we're using password
c.NotebookApp.token = ''
EOF
```

```bash
cat <<'EOF' > test_notebook.ipynb
{
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# What is Jupyter Notebook?\n",
    "\n",
    "Jupyter Notebook is an open-source web application that allows you to create and share documents that contain live code, equations, visualizations and 
narrative text. It's widely used for data cleaning and transformation, numerical simulation, statistical modeling, data visualization, machine learning, and 
much more.\n",
    "\n",
    "The notebook extends the console-based approach to interactive computing in a qualitatively new direction, providing a web-based application suitable for 
capturing the whole computation process: developing, documenting, and executing code, as well as communicating the results."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "\n",
    "# Define x values\n",
    "x = np.linspace(0, 2*np.pi, 100)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Define y values (sine wave)\n",
    "y = np.sin(x)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Plot the sine wave\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))\n",
    "plt.plot(x, y, label='sin(x)')\n",
    "plt.title('Simple Sine Wave')\n",
    "plt.xlabel('x')\n",
    "plt.ylabel('sin(x)')\n",
    "plt.grid(True)\n",
    "plt.legend()\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  }
 ],
```
